name: python-jsonpath

metadata.name: python-jsonpath
metadata.summary: JSONPath, JSON Pointer and JSON Patch for Python.

documentation:
Python JSONPath

A flexible JSONPath engine for Python.
We follow RFC 9535 and test against the JSONPath Compliance Test Suite.


--- **Table of Contents** - [Install](#install) - [Links](#links) - [Related projects](#related-projects) - [Examples](#examples) - [License](#license) ## Install Install Python JSONPath using [pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/getting-started/): ``` pip install python-jsonpath ``` Or [Pipenv](https://pipenv.pypa.io/en/latest/): ``` pipenv install -u python-jsonpath ``` Or from [conda-forge](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/python-jsonpath): ``` conda install -c conda-forge python-jsonpath ``` ## Links - Documentation: https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/. - JSONPath Syntax: https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/syntax/ - Change log: https://github.com/jg-rp/python-jsonpath/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md - PyPi: https://pypi.org/project/python-jsonpath - Source code: https://github.com/jg-rp/python-jsonpath - Issue tracker: https://github.com/jg-rp/python-jsonpath/issues ## Related projects - [Python JSONPath RFC 9535](https://github.com/jg-rp/python-jsonpath-rfc9535) - An implementation of JSONPath that follows RFC 9535 much more strictly. If you require maximum interoperability with JSONPath implemented in other languages - at the expense of extra features - choose python-jsonpath-rfc9535 over python-jsonpath. python-jsonpath-rfc9535 matches RFC 9535's JSONPath model internally and is careful to match the spec's terminology. It also includes utilities for verifying and testing the [JSONPath Compliance Test Suite](https://github.com/jsonpath-standard/jsonpath-compliance-test-suite). Most notably the nondeterministic behavior of some JSONPath selectors. - [JSON P3](https://github.com/jg-rp/json-p3) - RFC 9535 implemented in TypeScript. JSON P3 does not include all the non-standard features of Python JSONPath, but does define some optional [extra syntax](https://jg-rp.github.io/json-p3/guides/jsonpath-extra). ## Examples ### JSONPath ```python import jsonpath data = { "users": [ {"name": "Sue", "score": 100}, {"name": "John", "score": 86}, {"name": "Sally", "score": 84}, {"name": "Jane", "score": 55}, ] } user_names = jsonpath.findall("$.users[?@.score < 100].name", data) print(user_names) # ['John', 'Sally', 'Jane'] ``` ### JSON Pointer We include an [RFC 6901](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901) compliant implementation of JSON Pointer. See JSON Pointer [quick start](https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/quickstart/#pointerresolvepointer-data), [guide](https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/pointers/) and [API reference](https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/api/#jsonpath.JSONPointer) ```python from jsonpath import pointer data = { "users": [ {"name": "Sue", "score": 100}, {"name": "John", "score": 86}, {"name": "Sally", "score": 84}, {"name": "Jane", "score": 55}, ] } sue_score = pointer.resolve("/users/0/score", data) print(sue_score) # 100 jane_score = pointer.resolve(["users", 3, "score"], data) print(jane_score) # 55 ``` ### JSON Patch We also include an [RFC 6902](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6902) compliant implementation of JSON Patch. See JSON Patch [quick start](https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/quickstart/#patchapplypatch-data) and [API reference](https://jg-rp.github.io/python-jsonpath/api/#jsonpath.JSONPatch) ```python from jsonpath import patch patch_operations = [ {"op": "add", "path": "/some/foo", "value": {"foo": {}}}, {"op": "add", "path": "/some/foo", "value": {"bar": []}}, {"op": "copy", "from": "/some/other", "path": "/some/foo/else"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/some/foo/bar/-", "value": 1}, ] data = {"some": {"other": "thing"}} patch.apply(patch_operations, data) print(data) # {'some': {'other': 'thing', 'foo': {'bar': [1], 'else': 'thing'}}} ``` ## License `python-jsonpath` is distributed under the terms of the [MIT](https://spdx.org/licenses/MIT.html) license.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.length
documentation: The standard `length` function extension.

classes.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.length.Length

methods.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.length.Length.__call__
documentation:
Return an object's length.

If the object does not have a length, the special _Nothing_ value is
returned.
A type-aware implementation of the standard `length` function.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.arguments
documentation: Class-based function extension base.

functions.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.arguments.validate
documentation:
Generic validation of function extension arguments using introspection.

RFC 9535 requires us to reject paths that use filter functions with too
many or too few arguments.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.keys
documentation: The built-in `keys` function extension.

functions.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.keys.keys
documentation: Return an object's keys, or `None` if the object has no _keys_ method.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.search
documentation: The standard `search` function extension.

classes.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.search.Search

methods.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.search.Search.__call__
documentation: Return `True` if _string_ contains _pattern_, or `False` otherwise.
A type-aware implementation of the standard `search` function.

documentation: The standard `count` function extension.

The built-in `count` function.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions
documentation: noqa: D104

documentation: The type of a filter function argument or return value.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.typeof
documentation: A non-standard "typeof" filter function.

classes.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.typeof.TypeOf

methods.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.typeof.TypeOf.__call__
documentation:
Return the type of _obj_ as a string.

The strings returned from this function use JSON terminology, much
like the result of JavaScript's `typeof` operator.

A non-standard "typeof" filter function.

Arguments:
    single_number_type: If True, will return "number" for ints and floats,
        otherwise we'll use "int" and "float" respectively. Defaults to `True`.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.match
documentation: The standard `match` function extension.

classes.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.match.Match

methods.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.match.Match.__call__
documentation: Return `True` if _string_ matches _pattern_, or `False` otherwise.
A type-aware implementation of the standard `match` function.

modules.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.is_instance
documentation: A non-standard "isinstance" filter function.

classes.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.is_instance.IsInstance

methods.name: jsonpath.function_extensions.is_instance.IsInstance.__call__
documentation:
Return `True` if the type of _obj_ matches _t_.

This function allows _t_ to be one of several aliases for the real
Python "type". Some of these aliases follow JavaScript/JSON semantics.
A non-standard "isinstance" filter function.

modules.name: jsonpath.pointer
documentation: JSON Pointer. See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901.

functions.name: jsonpath.pointer.resolve
documentation:
Resolve JSON Pointer _pointer_ against _data_.

Args:
    pointer: A string representation of a JSON Pointer or an iterable of
        JSON Pointer parts.
    data: The target JSON "document" or equivalent Python objects.
    default: A default value to return if the pointer can't be resolved.
        against the given data.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing the pointer.
    uri_decode: If `True`, the pointer will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

Returns:
    The object in _data_ pointed to by this pointer.

Raises:
    JSONPointerIndexError: When attempting to access a sequence by
        an out of range index, unless a default is given.
    JSONPointerKeyError: If any mapping object along the path does not contain
        a specified key, unless a default is given.
    JSONPointerTypeError: When attempting to resolve a non-index string path
        part against a sequence, unless a default is given.

classes.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.resolve
documentation:
Resolve this pointer against _data_.

Args:
    data: The target JSON "document" or equivalent Python objects.
    default: A default value to return if the pointer can't be resolved
        against the given data.

Returns:
    The object in _data_ pointed to by this pointer.

Raises:
    JSONPointerIndexError: When attempting to access a sequence by
        an out of range index, unless a default is given.
    JSONPointerKeyError: If any mapping object along the path does not
        contain a specified key, unless a default is given.
    JSONPointerTypeError: When attempting to resolve a non-index string
        path part against a sequence, unless a default is given.

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.resolve_parent
documentation:
Resolve this pointer against _data_, return the object and its parent.

Args:
    data: The target JSON "document" or equivalent Python objects.

Returns:
    A `(parent, object)` tuple, where parent will be `None` if this
        pointer points to the root node in the document. If the parent
        exists but the last object does not, `(parent, UNDEFINED)` will
        be returned.

Raises:
    JSONPointerIndexError: When attempting to access a sequence by
        an out of range index, unless using the special `-` index.
    JSONPointerKeyError: If any mapping object along the path does not
        contain a specified key, unless it is the last part of the
        pointer.
    JSONPointerTypeError: When attempting to resolve a non-index string
        path part against a sequence.

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.from_match
documentation: Return a JSON Pointer for the path from a JSONPathMatch instance.

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.from_parts
documentation:
Build a JSON Pointer from _parts_.

Args:
    parts: The keys, indices and/or slices that make up a JSONPointer.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing the pointer.
    uri_decode: If `True`, the pointer will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

Returns:
    A new `JSONPointer` built from _parts_.

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.is_relative_to
documentation: Return _True_ if this pointer points to a child of _other_.

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.exists
documentation:
Return _True_ if this pointer can be resolved against _data_.

Note that `JSONPointer.resolve()` can return legitimate falsy values
that form part of the target JSON document. This method will return
`True` if a falsy value is found.

Args:
    data: The target JSON "document" or equivalent Python objects.

Returns:
    _True_ if this pointer can be resolved against _data_, or _False_
        otherwise.

**_New in version 0.9.0_**

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.parent
documentation:
Return this pointer's parent, as a new `JSONPointer`.

If this pointer points to the document root, _self_ is returned.

**_New in version 0.9.0_**

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.__truediv__
documentation:
Join this pointer with _other_.

_other_ is expected to be a JSON Pointer string, possibly without a
leading slash. If _other_ does have a leading slash, the previous
pointer is ignored and a new JSONPath is returned from _other_.

_other_ should not be a "Relative JSON Pointer".

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.join
documentation:
Join this pointer with _parts_.

Each part is expected to be a JSON Pointer string, possibly without a
leading slash. If a part does have a leading slash, the previous
pointer is ignored and a new `JSONPointer` is created, and processing of
remaining parts continues.

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.JSONPointer.to
documentation:
Return a new pointer relative to this pointer.

Args:
    rel: A `RelativeJSONPointer` or a string following "Relative JSON
        Pointer" syntax.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing the pointer.
    uri_decode: If `True`, the pointer will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

See https://www.ietf.org/id/draft-hha-relative-json-pointer-00.html

Identify a single, specific value in JSON-like data, as per RFC 6901.

Args:
    pointer: A string representation of a JSON Pointer.
    parts: The keys, indices and/or slices that make up a JSON Pointer. If
        given, it is assumed that the parts have already been parsed by the
        JSONPath parser. `unicode_escape` and `uri_decode` are ignored if
        _parts_ is given.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing the pointer.
    uri_decode: If `True`, the pointer will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

Attributes:
    keys_selector (str): The non-standard token used to target a mapping
        key or name.
    max_int_index (int): The maximum integer allowed when resolving array
        items by index. Defaults to `(2**53) - 1`.
    min_int_index (int): The minimum integer allowed when resolving array
        items by index. Defaults to `-(2**53) + 1`.

classes.name: jsonpath.pointer.RelativeJSONPointer

methods.name: jsonpath.pointer.RelativeJSONPointer.to
documentation:
Return a new JSONPointer relative to _pointer_.

Args:
    pointer: A `JSONPointer` instance or a string following JSON
        Pointer syntax.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing the pointer.
    uri_decode: If `True`, the pointer will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

A Relative JSON Pointer.

See https://www.ietf.org/id/draft-hha-relative-json-pointer-00.html

Args:
    rel: A string following Relative JSON Pointer syntax.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing the pointer.
    uri_decode: If `True`, the pointer will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

modules.name: jsonpath.patch
documentation: JSON Patch, as per RFC 6902.

functions.name: jsonpath.patch.apply
documentation:
Apply the JSON Patch _patch_ to _data_.

If _data_ is a string or file-like object, it will be loaded with
_json.loads_. Otherwise _data_ should be a JSON-like data structure and
will be **modified in-place**.

When modifying _data_ in-place, we return modified data too. This is
to allow for replacing _data's_ root element, which is allowed by some
patch operations.

Arguments:
    patch: A JSON Patch formatted document or equivalent Python objects.
    data: The target JSON "document" or equivalent Python objects.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing JSON pointers.
    uri_decode: If `True`, JSON pointers will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

Returns:
    Modified input data.

Raises:
    JSONPatchError: When a patch operation fails.
    JSONPatchTestFailure: When a _test_ operation does not pass.
        `JSONPatchTestFailure` is a subclass of `JSONPatchError`.

classes.name: jsonpath.patch.Op

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.Op.apply
documentation: Apply this patch operation to _data_.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.Op.asdict
documentation: Return a dictionary representation of this operation.
One of the JSON Patch operations.

classes.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAdd

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAdd.apply
documentation: Apply this patch operation to _data_.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAdd.asdict
documentation: Return a dictionary representation of this operation.
The JSON Patch _add_ operation.

classes.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAddNe

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAddNe.apply
documentation: Apply this patch operation to _data_.

A non-standard _add if not exists_ operation.

This is like _OpAdd_, but only adds object/dict keys/values if they key does
not already exist.

**New in version 1.2.0**

classes.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAddAp

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.OpAddAp.apply
documentation: Apply this patch operation to _data_.

A non-standard add operation that appends to arrays/lists .

This is like _OpAdd_, but assumes an index of "-" if the path can not
be resolved.

**New in version 1.2.0**

classes.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.add
documentation:
Append an _add_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    value: The object to add.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.addne
documentation:
Append an _addne_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    value: The object to add.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.addap
documentation:
Append an _addap_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    value: The object to add.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.remove
documentation:
Append a _remove_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.replace
documentation:
Append a _replace_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    value: The object to add.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.move
documentation:
Append a _move_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    from_: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.copy
documentation:
Append a _copy_ operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    from_: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.test
documentation:
Append a test operation to this patch.

Arguments:
    path: A string representation of a JSON Pointer, or one that has
        already been parsed.
    value: The object to test.

Returns:
    This `JSONPatch` instance, so we can build a JSON Patch by chaining
        calls to JSON Patch operation methods.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.apply
documentation:
Apply all operations from this patch to _data_.

If _data_ is a string or file-like object, it will be loaded with
_json.loads_. Otherwise _data_ should be a JSON-like data structure and
will be modified in place.

When modifying _data_ in place, we return modified data too. This is
to allow for replacing _data's_ root element, which is allowed by some
patch operations.

Arguments:
    data: The target JSON "document" or equivalent Python objects.

Returns:
    Modified input data.

Raises:
    JSONPatchError: When a patch operation fails.
    JSONPatchTestFailure: When a _test_ operation does not pass.
        `JSONPatchTestFailure` is a subclass of `JSONPatchError`.

methods.name: jsonpath.patch.JSONPatch.asdicts
documentation: Return a list of this patch's operations as dictionaries.

Modify JSON-like data with JSON Patch.

RFC 6902 defines operations to manipulate a JSON document. `JSONPatch`
supports parsing and applying standard JSON Patch formatted operations,
and provides a Python builder API following the same semantics as RFC 6902.

Arguments:
    ops: A JSON Patch formatted document or equivalent Python objects.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, UTF-16 escape sequences will be decoded
        before parsing JSON pointers.
    uri_decode: If `True`, JSON pointers will be unescaped using _urllib_
        before being parsed.

Raises:
    JSONPatchError: If _ops_ is given and any of the provided operations
        is malformed.

modules.name: jsonpath.parse
documentation: The default JSONPath parser.

classes.name: jsonpath.parse.Parser

methods.name: jsonpath.parse.Parser.parse
documentation: Parse a JSONPath from a stream of tokens.

methods.name: jsonpath.parse.Parser.parse_path
documentation: Parse a top-level JSONPath, or one that is nested in a filter.

methods.name: jsonpath.parse.Parser.parse_slice
documentation: Parse a slice JSONPath expression from a stream of tokens.

methods.name: jsonpath.parse.Parser.parse_selector_list
documentation: Parse a comma separated list JSONPath selectors from a stream of tokens.
A JSONPath parser bound to a JSONPathEnvironment.

modules.name: jsonpath.env
documentation: Core JSONPath configuration object.

classes.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.compile
documentation:
Prepare a path string ready for repeated matching against different data.

Arguments:
    path: A JSONPath as a string.

Returns:
    A `JSONPath` or `CompoundJSONPath`, ready to match against some data.
        Expect a `CompoundJSONPath` if the path string uses the _union_ or
        _intersection_ operators.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If _path_ is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If filter functions are given arguments of an
        unacceptable type.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.findall
documentation:
Find all objects in _data_ matching the JSONPath _path_.

If _data_ is a string or a file-like objects, it will be loaded
using `json.loads()` and the default `JSONDecoder`.

Arguments:
    path: The JSONPath as a string.
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    A list of matched objects. If there are no matches, the list will
        be empty.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.finditer
documentation:
Generate `JSONPathMatch` objects for each match of _path_ in _data_.

If _data_ is a string or a file-like objects, it will be loaded using
`json.loads()` and the default `JSONDecoder`.

Arguments:
    path: The JSONPath as a string.
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    An iterator yielding `JSONPathMatch` objects for each match.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.match
documentation:
Return a `JSONPathMatch` instance for the first object found in _data_.

`None` is returned if there are no matches.

Arguments:
    path: The JSONPath as a string.
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    A `JSONPathMatch` object for the first match, or `None` if there were
        no matches.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.query
documentation:
Return a `Query` iterator over matches found by applying _path_ to _data_.

`Query` objects are iterable.

```
for match in jsonpath.query("$.foo..bar", data):
    ...
```

You can skip and limit results with `Query.skip()` and `Query.limit()`.

```
matches = (
    jsonpath.query("$.foo..bar", data)
    .skip(5)
    .limit(10)
)

for match in matches
    ...
```

`Query.tail()` will get the last _n_ results.

```
for match in jsonpath.query("$.foo..bar", data).tail(5):
    ...
```

Get values for each match using `Query.values()`.

```
for obj in jsonpath.query("$.foo..bar", data).limit(5).values():
    ...
```

Arguments:
    path: The JSONPath as a string.
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    A query iterator.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.findall_async
documentation: An async version of `findall()`.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.finditer_async
documentation: An async version of `finditer()`.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.setup_function_extensions
documentation: Initialize function extensions.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.validate_function_extension_signature
documentation:
Compile-time validation of function extension arguments.

RFC 9535 requires us to reject paths that use filter functions with
too many or too few arguments.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.check_well_typedness
documentation: Check the well-typedness of a function's arguments at compile-time.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment._function_return_type
documentation:
Return the type returned from a filter function.

If _expr_ is not a `FunctionExtension` or the registered function definition is
not type-aware, return `None`.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.getitem
documentation:
Sequence and mapping item getter used throughout JSONPath resolution.

The default implementation of `getitem` simply calls `operators.getitem()`
from Python's standard library. Same as `obj[key]`.

Arguments:
    obj: A mapping or sequence that might contain _key_.
    key: A mapping key, sequence index or sequence slice.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.getitem_async
documentation: An async sequence and mapping item getter.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.is_truthy
documentation:
Test for truthiness when evaluating JSONPath filter expressions.

In some cases, RFC 9535 requires us to test for existence rather than
truthiness. So the default implementation returns `True` for empty
collections and `None`. The special `UNDEFINED` object means that
_obj_ was missing, as opposed to an explicit `None`.

Arguments:
    obj: Any object.

Returns:
    `True` if the object exists and is not `False` or `0`.

methods.name: jsonpath.env.JSONPathEnvironment.compare
documentation:
Object comparison within JSONPath filters.

Override this to customize filter expression comparison operator
behavior.

Args:
    left: The left hand side of the comparison expression.
    operator: The comparison expression's operator.
    right: The right hand side of the comparison expression.

Returns:
    `True` if the comparison between _left_ and _right_, with the
    given _operator_, is truthy. `False` otherwise.

JSONPath configuration.

This class contains settings for path tokenization, parsing and resolution
behavior, plus convenience methods for matching an unparsed path to some
data.

Most applications will want to create a single `JSONPathEnvironment`, or
use `jsonpath.compile()`, `jsonpath.findall()`, etc. from the package-level
default environment.

## Environment customization

Environment customization is achieved by subclassing `JSONPathEnvironment`
and overriding class attributes and/or methods. Some of these
customizations include:

- Changing the root (`$`), self (`@`) or filter context (`_`) token with
  class attributes `root_token`, `self_token` and `filter_context_token`.
- Registering a custom lexer or parser with the class attributes
  `lexer_class` or `parser_class`. `lexer_class` must be a subclass of
  [`Lexer`]() and `parser_class` must be a subclass of [`Parser`]().
- Setup built-in function extensions by overriding
  `setup_function_extensions()`
- Hook in to mapping and sequence item getting by overriding `getitem()`.
- Change filter comparison operator behavior by overriding `compare()`.

Arguments:
    filter_caching (bool): If `True`, filter expressions will be cached
        where possible.
    unicode_escape: If `True`, decode UTF-16 escape sequences found in
        JSONPath string literals.
    well_typed: Control well-typedness checks on filter function expressions.
        If `True` (the default), JSONPath expressions are checked for
        well-typedness as compile time.

        **New in version 0.10.0**

## Class attributes

Attributes:
    fake_root_token (str): The pattern used to select a "fake" root node, one level
        above the real root node.
    filter_context_token (str): The pattern used to select extra filter context
        data. Defaults to `"_"`.
    intersection_token (str): The pattern used as the intersection operator.
        Defaults to `"&"`.
    key_token (str): The pattern used to identify the current key or index when
        filtering a, mapping or sequence. Defaults to `"#"`.
    keys_selector_token (str): The pattern used as the "keys" selector. Defaults to
        `"~"`.
    lexer_class: The lexer to use when tokenizing path strings.
    max_int_index (int): The maximum integer allowed when selecting array items by
        index. Defaults to `(2**53) - 1`.
    min_int_index (int): The minimum integer allowed when selecting array items by
        index. Defaults to `-(2**53) + 1`.
    parser_class: The parser to use when parsing tokens from the lexer.
    root_token (str): The pattern used to select the root node in a JSON document.
        Defaults to `"$"`.
    self_token (str): The pattern used to select the current node in a JSON
        document. Defaults to `"@"`
    union_token (str): The pattern used as the union operator. Defaults to `"|"`.

modules.name: jsonpath.exceptions
documentation: JSONPath exceptions.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPathError
documentation:
Base exception for all errors.

Arguments:
    args: Arguments passed to `Exception`.
    token: The token that caused the error.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPathSyntaxError
documentation:
An exception raised when parsing a JSONPath string.

Arguments:
    args: Arguments passed to `Exception`.
    token: The token that caused the error.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPathTypeError
documentation:
An exception raised due to a type error.

This should only occur at when evaluating filter expressions.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPathIndexError
documentation:
An exception raised when an array index is out of range.

Arguments:
    args: Arguments passed to `Exception`.
    token: The token that caused the error.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPathNameError
documentation:
An exception raised when an unknown function extension is called.

Arguments:
    args: Arguments passed to `Exception`.
    token: The token that caused the error.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPointerError
documentation: Base class for all JSON Pointer errors.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPointerEncodeError
documentation: An exception raised when a JSONPathMatch can't be encoded to a JSON Pointer.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPointerResolutionError
documentation: Base exception for those that can be raised during pointer resolution.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPointerIndexError
documentation: An exception raised when an array index is out of range.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPointerKeyError
documentation: An exception raised when a pointer references a mapping with a missing key.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPointerTypeError
documentation: An exception raised when a pointer resolves a string against a sequence.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.RelativeJSONPointerError
documentation: Base class for all Relative JSON Pointer errors.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.RelativeJSONPointerIndexError
documentation: An exception raised when modifying a pointer index out of range.

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.RelativeJSONPointerSyntaxError

classes.name: jsonpath.exceptions.JSONPatchError

modules.name: jsonpath.selectors
documentation: JSONPath segments and selectors, as returned from `Parser.parse`.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.JSONPathSelector

methods.name: jsonpath.selectors.JSONPathSelector.resolve
documentation:
Apply the segment/selector to each node in _matches_.

Arguments:
    matches: Nodes matched by preceding segments/selectors. This is like
        a lazy _NodeList_, as described in RFC 9535, but each match carries
        more than the node's value and location.

Returns:
    The `JSONPathMatch` instances created by applying this selector to each
    preceding node.

methods.name: jsonpath.selectors.JSONPathSelector.resolve_async
documentation: An async version of `resolve`.
Base class for all JSONPath segments and selectors.
ruff: noqa: D102

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.PropertySelector
documentation: A shorthand or bracketed property selector.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.IndexSelector
documentation:
Select an element from an array by index.

Considering we don't require mapping (JSON object) keys/properties to
be quoted, and that we support mappings with numeric keys, we also check
to see if the "index" is a mapping key, which is non-standard.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.KeysSelector
documentation:
Select mapping/object keys/properties.

NOTE: This is a non-standard selector.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.SliceSelector
documentation: Sequence slicing selector.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.WildSelector
documentation: Select all items from a sequence/array or values from a mapping/object.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.RecursiveDescentSelector
documentation:
A JSONPath selector that visits all nodes recursively.

NOTE: Strictly this is a "segment", not a "selector".

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.ListSelector
documentation:
A bracketed list of selectors, the results of which are concatenated together.

NOTE: Strictly this is a "segment", not a "selector".

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.Filter
documentation: Filter sequence/array items or mapping/object values with a filter expression.

classes.name: jsonpath.selectors.FilterContext
documentation: Contextual information and data for evaluating a filter expression.

Update this expression's child expressions.

_children_ is assumed to have the same number of items as is returned
by _self.children_, and in the same order.

modules.name: jsonpath.match
documentation: The JSONPath match object, as returned from `JSONPath.finditer()`.

classes.name: jsonpath.match.JSONPathMatch

methods.name: jsonpath.match.JSONPathMatch.add_child
documentation: Append one or more children to this match.

methods.name: jsonpath.match.JSONPathMatch.filter_context
documentation: Return filter context data for this match.

methods.name: jsonpath.match.JSONPathMatch.pointer
documentation: Return a `JSONPointer` pointing to this match's path.

methods.name: jsonpath.match.JSONPathMatch.value
documentation: Return the value associated with this match/node.

A matched object with a concrete path.

Attributes:
    children: Matched child nodes. This will only be populated after
        all children have been visited, usually by using `findall()`
        or `list(finditer())`.
    obj: The matched object.
    parent: The immediate parent to this match in the JSON document.
        If this is the root node, _parent_ will be `None`.
    path: The canonical string representation of the path to this match.
    parts: The keys, indices and/or slices that make up the path to this
        match.
    root: A reference to the root node in the JSON document.

classes.name: jsonpath.match.NodeList

methods.name: jsonpath.match.NodeList.values
documentation: Return the values from this node list.

methods.name: jsonpath.match.NodeList.values_or_singular
documentation: Return the values from this node list.

methods.name: jsonpath.match.NodeList.empty
documentation: Return `True` if this node list is empty.
List of JSONPathMatch objects, analogous to the spec's nodelist.

modules.name: jsonpath.fluent_api
documentation: A fluent API for working with `JSONPathMatch` iterators.

functions.name: jsonpath.fluent_api._fix_sparse_arrays
documentation: Fix sparse arrays (dictionaries with integer keys).

classes.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Projection

class_variables.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Projection.RELATIVE
documentation:
The default projection. Selections include parent arrays and objects relative
to the JSONPathMatch.

class_variables.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Projection.ROOT
documentation: Selections include parent arrays and objects relative to the root JSON value.

class_variables.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Projection.FLAT
documentation:
All selections are appended to a new array/list, without arrays and objects
on the path to the selected value.
Projection style used by `Query.select()`.

classes.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.limit
documentation:
Limit the query iterator to at most _n_ matches.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.head
documentation:
Limit the query iterator to at most the first _n_ matches.

`head()` is an alias for `limit()`.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.first
documentation:
Limit the query iterator to at most the first _n_ matches.

`first()` is an alias for `limit()`.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.drop
documentation:
Skip up to _n_ matches from the query iterator.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.skip
documentation:
Skip up to _n_ matches from the query iterator.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.tail
documentation:
Drop matches up to the last _n_ matches from the iterator.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.last
documentation:
Drop up to the last _n_ matches from the iterator.

`last()` is an alias for `tail()`.

Raises:
    ValueError: If _n_ < 0.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.values
documentation: Return an iterable of objects associated with each match.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.locations
documentation: Return an iterable of normalized paths, one for each match.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.items
documentation: Return an iterable of (path, object) tuples, one for each match.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.pointers
documentation: Return an iterable of JSONPointers, one for each match.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.first_one
documentation: Return the first `JSONPathMatch` or `None` if there were no matches.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.one
documentation:
Return the first `JSONPathMatch` or `None` if there were no matches.

`one()` is an alias for `first_one()`.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.last_one
documentation: Return the last `JSONPathMatch` or `None` if there were no matches.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.tee
documentation:
Return _n_ independent queries by teeing this query's iterator.

It is not safe to use a `Query` instance after calling `tee()`.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.take
documentation:
Return a new query iterating over the next _n_ matches.

It is safe to continue using this query after calling take.

methods.name: jsonpath.fluent_api.Query.select
documentation:
Query projection using relative JSONPaths.

Arguments:
    expressions: One or more JSONPath query expressions to select relative
        to each match in this query iterator.
    projection: The style of projection used when selecting values. Can be
        one of `Projection.RELATIVE`, `Projection.ROOT` or `Projection.FLAT`.
        Defaults to `Projection.RELATIVE`.

Returns:
    An iterable of objects built from selecting _expressions_ relative to
        each match from the current query.

**New in version 1.2.0**

A fluent API for managing `JSONPathMatch` iterators.

Usually you'll want to use `jsonpath.query()` or `JSONPathEnvironment.query()`
to create instances of `Query` rather than instantiating `Query` directly.

Arguments:
    it: A `JSONPathMatch` iterable, as you'd get from `jsonpath.finditer()` or
        `JSONPathEnvironment.finditer()`.

**New in version 1.1.0**

modules.name: jsonpath.lex
documentation: JSONPath tokenization.

classes.name: jsonpath.lex.Lexer

methods.name: jsonpath.lex.Lexer.compile_rules
documentation: Prepare regular expression rules.

methods.name: jsonpath.lex.Lexer.tokenize
documentation: Generate a sequence of tokens from a JSONPath string.

Tokenize a JSONPath string.

Some customization can be achieved by subclassing _Lexer_ and setting
class attributes. Then setting `lexer_class` on a `JSONPathEnvironment`.

Attributes:
    key_pattern: The regular expression pattern used to match mapping
        keys/properties.
    logical_not_pattern: The regular expression pattern used to match
        logical negation tokens. By default, `not` and `!` are
        equivalent.
    logical_and_pattern: The regular expression pattern used to match
        logical _and_ tokens. By default, `and` and `&&` are equivalent.
    logical_or_pattern: The regular expression pattern used to match
        logical _or_ tokens. By default, `or` and `||` are equivalent.

modules.name: jsonpath.token
documentation: JSONPath tokens.

classes.name: jsonpath.token.Token

methods.name: jsonpath.token.Token.position
documentation: Return the line and column number for the start of this token.

A token, as returned from `lex.Lexer.tokenize()`.

Attributes:
    kind (str): The token's type. It is always one of the constants defined
        in _jsonpath.token.py_.
    value (str): The _path_ substring containing text for the token.
    index (str): The index at which _value_ starts in _path_.
    path (str): A reference to the complete JSONPath string from which this
        token derives.

modules.name: jsonpath.path
documentation: noqa: D100

classes.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.findall
documentation:
Find all objects in `data` matching the given JSONPath `path`.

If `data` is a string or a file-like objects, it will be loaded
using `json.loads()` and the default `JSONDecoder`.

Arguments:
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    A list of matched objects. If there are no matches, the list will
    be empty.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.finditer
documentation:
Generate `JSONPathMatch` objects for each match.

If `data` is a string or a file-like objects, it will be loaded
using `json.loads()` and the default `JSONDecoder`.

Arguments:
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    An iterator yielding `JSONPathMatch` objects for each match.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.findall_async
documentation: An async version of `findall()`.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.finditer_async
documentation: An async version of `finditer()`.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.match
documentation:
Return a `JSONPathMatch` instance for the first object found in _data_.

`None` is returned if there are no matches.

Arguments:
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    A `JSONPathMatch` object for the first match, or `None` if there were
        no matches.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.query
documentation:
Return a `Query` iterator over matches found by applying this path to _data_.

Arguments:
    data: A JSON document or Python object implementing the `Sequence`
        or `Mapping` interfaces.
    filter_context: Arbitrary data made available to filters using
        the _filter context_ selector.

Returns:
    A query iterator.

Raises:
    JSONPathSyntaxError: If the path is invalid.
    JSONPathTypeError: If a filter expression attempts to use types in
        an incompatible way.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.empty
documentation: Return `True` if this path has no selectors.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.JSONPath.singular_query
documentation: Return `True` if this JSONPath query is a singular query.

A compiled JSONPath ready to be applied to a JSON string or Python object.

Arguments:
    env: The `JSONPathEnvironment` this path is bound to.
    selectors: An iterable of `JSONPathSelector` objects, as generated by
        a `Parser`.
    fake_root: Indicates if target JSON values should be wrapped in a single-
        element array, so as to make the target root value selectable.


Attributes:
    env: The `JSONPathEnvironment` this path is bound to.
    selectors: The `JSONPathSelector` instances that make up this path.

methods.name: jsonpath.path.CompoundJSONPath.intersection
documentation: Intersection of this path and another path.
Multiple `JSONPath`s combined.
